claim_text: Whereas most HP13C cancer studies have focused on Lac27,28,29,34 , we hypothesized that the 13 C Lac/Bic ratio is a surrogate marker for GLY/OXPHOS5,35,36 .

sub_claims.text: Datta et al. 2019 uses the 13C Lac/Bic ratio (from hyperpolarized [1-13C]pyruvate MRSI) as a surrogate metric for the relative balance of glycolysis (Lac) versus oxidative phosphorylation (Bic) in a rat C6 glioma cancer model, directly supporting the manuscript's claim that the 13C Lac/Bic ratio is a surrogate marker for GLY/OXPHOS.

evidence.snippet: The aim of this longitudinal study of glioblastoma in a rat model was to determine whether the ratio of lactate (surrogate marker for glycolysis) to bicarbonate (for oxidative phosphorylation), as measured via in vivo magnetic resonance imaging of hyperpolarized 13C-labeled pyruvate accurately predicts survival.

13C Lactate to 13C Bicarbonate ratio (Lac/Bic) at 48 hours post-treatment is highly predictive of survival (p = 0.003). These results suggest a potential role for the 13C Lac/Bic ratio serving as a valuable measure of tumor metabolism and predicting therapeutic response.

Pyruvate... occupies a key nodal point in brain glucose metabolism in which it is either converted to lactate (Lac, a surrogate for GLY) or acetyl CoA + CO2 (generating bicarbonate, Bic, in the process; reflecting OXPHOS), enabling the measurement of GLY and OXPHOS indirectly. We proposed the ratio of 13C Lac to 13C Bic (Lac/Bic) as a biomarker of tumor therapeutic response as it reflects the relative preponderance of these metabolic pathways.

Measurements of in-vivo 13C Lac and Bic labeling via hyperpolarized [1-13C]Pyr have been suggested as surrogate markers for the underlying GLY and OXPHOS metabolic pathways respectively, and the Lac/Bic ratio a metric representing the relative balance of these processes. Results from this study support this hypothesis and further demonstrate that a decrease in the Lac/Bic ratio is a robust predictor of treatment response in this animal model when treated with anti-VEGF therapy.

all 6 deceased animals showed an increase in tumor Lac/Bic at 48 hrs with the mean value changing from 11.077 to 17.767. In contrast, 6 of the 7 B20.4.1.1 treated rats that survived showed a decrease in tumor Lac/Bic ratio (mean decreased from 10.767 to 8.862)... Hence, Lac/Bic ratios from the healthy and tumor ROIs before and after treatment clearly show differences between the deceased and survivor rats...

sub_claims.nuance: Datta 2019 is an explicit primary source for the 13C Lac/Bic-as-GLY/OXPHOS-surrogate framing: the abstract, introduction, and discussion all state the Lac/Bic ratio reflects the relative balance of glycolysis vs oxidative phosphorylation, and the paper's primary longitudinal result (Lac/Bic at 48 hours post-treatment predicts survival, p=0.003) is built on that framing - i.e., Lac/Bic is the paper's primary readout, not a secondary metric. Direct match to the manuscript's claim.

phrasings_tried: 13C Lac/Bic ratio as surrogate for GLY/OXPHOS balance
lactate surrogate for glycolysis, bicarbonate surrogate for oxidative phosphorylation in hyperpolarized 13C pyruvate
hyperpolarized [1-13C]pyruvate Lac/Bic predicts tumor response in glioma
Warburg / metabolic reprogramming measured by 13C Lac/Bic ratio
C6 glioma anti-VEGF treatment Lac/Bic longitudinal HP13C
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: Datta 2019 credits its own earlier conceptual-framing work (self-cites refs [17], [19] - prior Datta/Spielman papers proposing Lac/Bic as biomarker) for first proposing the Lac/Bic-as-GLY/OXPHOS framing. Datta 2019 itself operationalizes and tests the hypothesis in a longitudinal glioma survival study, making it a legitimate primary citation for the manuscript's claim (not an indirect relay).
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript cites datta2019 as supporting '13C Lac/Bic ratio is a surrogate marker for GLY/OXPHOS'. Datta 2019's abstract, introduction and discussion all explicitly state this framing and operationalize it experimentally. Context is an exact match.

sibling_verdicts.excerpt: Co-cited (with datta2019, park2016) for the Lac/Bic-as-GLY/OXPHOS-surrogate clause in the manuscript; expected to provide complementary support for the surrogate-marker framing.
Co-cited for the same GLY/OXPHOS-surrogate clause; expected to support the Lac/Bic framing.
Cited for the prior clause ('most HP13C cancer studies have focused on Lac') - see C043; does not anchor the Lac/Bic-as-GLY/OXPHOS clause that datta2019 anchors.
Cited for the Lac-focused-prior-work clause, not the GLY/OXPHOS-surrogate clause.
